title: OpenAI's Plan To Merge Chat And Agents — With Greg Brockman
summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI's president and co-founder, discusses OpenAI's trajectory toward merging chat and agentic interfaces into persistent agents. He argues that compute will be the decisive factor in the AI race and that interfaces will eventually dissolve into agents acting autonomously on users' behalf. He also covers competitive dynamics with Microsoft, the evolution of pricing, and AI applications in health.
writeup: Greg Brockman, OpenAI's president and co-founder, lays out a trajectory in which chat and agentic interfaces converge into persistent agents that act autonomously on a user's behalf, with interfaces eventually dissolving into those agents. He argues that compute will be the decisive factor in the AI race. The conversation also addresses competitive dynamics with Microsoft, the evolution of pricing, and AI applications in health.
